purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Approved core and UI story outputs
Existing package manifest, plugin manifest, CLI entry point, documentation, and test harness
outputs: observe CLI command and packaged runtime integration
Installed-artifact smoke tests and user documentation
Trace narrative support for pre-generated Codex explanations with safe fallback for legacy records
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
npm test and npm run check pass; npm pack includes all observatory assets.
An isolated tarball smoke test launches from installed package paths and serves health, model, and UI successfully.
Existing commands retain compatible behavior and help documents observe options.

notes: Use modular browser-native ES modules and code-native UI rather than adding a build tool.
Use browser QA only against the local loopback observatory.

contextualization.summary: Integrate Change Observatory into the published plugin so an installed copy can launch it with agentic-sdlc observe --root <project>, without a separate checkout, build, hosted service, or manual asset copy.

context_sources.excerpt: id REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 kind requirement schema_version requirement v1 title Install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory summary Provide a local visual application shipped inside the Agentic SDLC plugin that reconstructs what was requested decided changed verified and released across stories phases contracts and iterations for technical and non-technical readers. status proposed acceptance_criteria After plugin installation agentic-sdlc observe --root <project> launches the included application without a separate build or cloud service. The overview answers What was asked? What changed? and Why was it decided? from canonical .sdlc evidence and links every claim to source records. The timeline shows story iterations across Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release distinguishing recorded inferred missing blocked and complete states. Contracts approvals decisions assumptions risks changed files tests gates handoffs sync events and release evidence are navigable with raw-record access. Natural-language explanations are clearly labeled as generated contain no private chain-of-thought and expose only stored rationale inputs outputs alternatives and evidence. T

# Agentic SDLC Codex Plugin Agentic SDLC 0.6.0 gives Codex a guided way to understand an existing software project and deliver a verified technical or functional assessment. The normal experience is intentionally simple Codex explains what it inferred proposes the work in plain language creates the requested real file verifies it and returns an auditable result. Project state stays in the target repository under `.sdlc/`. The plugin installation contains only reusable skills templates schemas and the cross-platform Node.js CLI. ## Documentation Map - Documentation home (docs/README.md) — choose the right guide without knowing the internal record names. - How It Works (docs/how-it-works.md) — the complete two-checkpoint journey state transitions authorization verification and recovery. - Autonomy Limits and Metering (docs/limits-and-metering.md) — concrete time step token cost reserve warning and stop-policy examples. - Assessment Interactions (docs/agent-interactions.md) — the precise contract used for every user question. - Portable Installation (docs/portable-install.md) — installation update diagnosis and recovery on supported platforms. ## Quick Start Install from the `aantenor

questions.question: What is the install-time user path?
questions.answer: Install the plugin, run agentic-sdlc observe --root <project>, receive a loopback URL, and open the bundled application automatically unless --no-open is set.

questions.question: How is package completeness proven?
questions.answer: Pack the npm artifact, install or execute from the tarball in an isolated temporary project, launch, query health/data/assets, and terminate cleanly.

constraints: Bundle every required runtime and UI asset through package.json files; no postinstall or generated frontend build.
Support --host only as loopback, --port with 0 for an ephemeral port, --no-open for automation, and graceful shutdown.
Do not change unrelated CLI behavior or require secrets, cloud access, deployment, or package installation.
assumptions: Opening a browser uses a cross-platform best-effort built-in OS command and failure to open does not terminate the server.

approvals.summary: Freshness-only reapproval under Antonio's unchanged delegated Change Observatory scope after the story-to-contract reference was persisted; contracted behavior and exclusions are unchanged.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Freshness-only continuation of the already delegated Change Observatory delivery: reapprove the same three implementation contracts after refreshing their self-referential story context, then confirm the same three task starts; no material scope change and no install, external access, deployment, secrets, destructive actions, or unrelated work.
